title: UV super-renormalisability of the scalar Brazovskii functional
statement: In d=3 with K(q)=mu^2+Y(q^2-q0^2)^2, mu^2>0, every connected diagram with V>=1,I>=1 has superficial degree D=3-3V-I<0; by Weinberg's theorem the perturbative expansion is UV- and IR-finite. NO UV-divergent counterterm is required; a finite normal ordering is an optional scheme choice. (UV half of A3; the continuum limit of correlators is the separate open card A3-PERTURBATIVE-CONTINUUM-CORRELATORS.)
scope: NARROW (matching A2): one scalar field, d=3, quartic kernel, mu^2>0; PERTURBATIVE power counting, UV-finiteness ONLY. D=(d-4)I-dV+d<0 for d<=3 (d=4 marginal). NOT the continuum limit of correlators (separate card); NOT constructive; NOT full Class-II; NOT mu^2<0. Kernel hypothesis Y>0 (4th-order ellipticity / q^-4 decay) is made explicit in the consolidation v1.1.
falsifier: A connected diagram with V>=1,I>=1 and D>=0 (would need d>=4 or a softer-than-q^4 kernel); or a UV/IR divergence at mu^2>0.

reproduction.expected: 6/6 PASS (exit 0); D=3-3V-I<0, q^-4 decay, finite tadpole 0.767, no IR div, a->0 Riemann convergence

no_overclaim: The continuum limit of correlation functions (separate card A3-PERTURBATIVE-CONTINUUM-CORRELATORS); any constructive/non-perturbative statement; full Class-II; mu^2<0; treating the finite tadpole as a required counterterm (it is an optional scheme choice).
next_action: Operator sign-off; then close A3-GRAPHWISE-CONVERGENCE (the graphwise lemma) for the correlators card.
notes: A3 split 2026-06-23 (operator review): this card carries ONLY the UV super-renormalisability result (power counting + Weinberg), T6 PROVED CONDITIONAL APPROVED by operator 2026-06-23. The stronger 'all correlators -> cutoff-independent continuum limit' moved to A3-PERTURBATIVE-CONTINUUM-CORRELATORS (open; needs the measure definition + graphwise regulator-convergence lemma). Renamed from A3-RENORMALISATION. Reproduction bundle (T6, referee-grade, MANIFEST sha256): claims/A3-UV-SUPERRENORMALISABILITY/bundle/A3-Renormalisation-Foundation-260623. Consolidation v1.1 (paper-grade): Y>0 explicit; constants written generally (C_*(lambda,gamma), C(mu^2,Y,q0)) with numerics as production-anchor checks. Re-linked 2026-06-23 to A1-SCALAR-ANALYTIC-BRANCH: the positivity hypothesis is the SHELL mass m_sh^2>0 (= old mu^2 = K(q_star)), NOT the zero-momentum r=K(0) nor the solver template's mu2=r. The current Class-II/condensate solver config is a mock (A1-PRODUCTION-KERNEL-MANIFEST fails) and is NOT cited as this theorem's numerical implementation.
